  ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/transient-prancing-peach.md · /plan to edit
     Fix GeminiExtractor: Strip UI Markers and Fix Message Boundaries
                                                                                                                                                                                                            
     Context
                                                                                                                                                                                                            
     The AX tree regression test infrastructure is in place with 10 fixtures across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Terminal, and Warp. All 480 tests pass, but the extraction quality for Gemini is broken —    
     the expected output files contain corrupted text because the extractor itself produces bad output.

     Two issues confirmed by reviewing the AX tree fixtures against expected outputs:

     1. Gemini: "You said" / "Gemini said" markers leak into message text — every extracted turn is prefixed with "You said\n" and/or suffixed with "\nGemini said"
     2. Gemini: Message boundaries are wrong — the extractor treats entire message pairs (user + response) as single messages, uses fragile index-based alternation for speaker detection, and loses
     Gemini response content entirely

     Claude and ChatGPT extractors are working correctly. Claude has minor missing bold labels ("Government bonds", "Corporate bonds") but this is an AX tree limitation (empty AXStrongStyleGroup
     elements), not an extractor bug.

     Root Cause

     In conversation_extractors.py:391-399, _extract_messages_from_container() iterates children of the "best group" and calls _collect_child_text() on each child. But each child is a message pair
     containing:

     AXGroup [message pair]
       └─ ... (nested groups) ...
          ├─ AXHeading title="You said <message>"
          │   ├─ AXStaticText value="You said"      ← marker leaks
          │   └─ AXGroup → AXStaticText "<message>"  ← actual user text
          ├─ AXHeading title="Gemini said"
          │   └─ AXStaticText value="Gemini said"    ← marker leaks
          └─ AXGroup (response content)
              └─ [Gemini's response text]             ← never extracted

     _collect_child_text() grabs everything including the markers. The i % 2 alternation assigns wrong speakers since each child is a pair, not an individual message.

     Plan

     Step 1: Rewrite _extract_messages_from_container() (conversation_extractors.py:344-401)

     Keep the existing best-group finding logic (lines 352-383). Replace the extraction loop (lines 389-401) with heading-based extraction:

     For each child of best_group, recursively search for AXHeading elements. Use their AXTitle to determine speaker and extract text:

     - "You said" headings: Extract user text from the heading's AXTitle by stripping the "You said " prefix. Also check the AXGroup child of the heading for multi-line content (prefer it when
     available and longer).
     - "Gemini said" headings: Find the sibling AXGroup after the heading in the same parent, collect text via _collect_child_text().

     Step 2: Add helper methods to GeminiExtractor

     _find_speaker_headings(element, max_depth=12) — Recursively finds AXHeading elements whose title starts with "You said" or "Gemini said". Returns list of (heading, title, parent) tuples. Bounded
      by depth and visit counter (200).

     _extract_user_text_from_heading(heading) — Gets user message from a "You said" heading:
     1. Strip "You said " prefix from AXTitle
     2. Also try _collect_child_text() on the AXGroup child (skipping the "You said" AXStaticText)
     3. Return the longer/more complete version

     _extract_response_after_heading(heading, parent) — Gets Gemini response from content following a "Gemini said" heading:
     1. Find the heading's index in parent's children
     2. Collect text from subsequent sibling groups via _collect_child_text()
     3. Stop at next AXHeading or end of children

     Step 3: Add text-level cleanup as safety net

     After extracting each turn's text, strip known marker patterns:
     - Strip "You said\n" prefix
     - Strip "\nGemini said" suffix
     - Strip "You said " prefix (no newline variant)

     This provides defense-in-depth if the structural approach misses a marker.

     Step 4: Regenerate expected output files

     Run python -m tests.generate_expected --all to regenerate all expected outputs with the fixed extractor. The Gemini files should now have:
     - gemini-current-chat.json: Clean user messages without markers (Gemini response turns only if content exists in tree)
     - gemini-browser-current-chat.json: Clean alternating User/Gemini turns with actual response text
     - gemini-new-chat.json: Unchanged (empty conversation)

     Step 5: Run regression tests

     Run ./venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v to verify all tests pass with the updated expected outputs.

     Files to Modify

     ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
     │                           File                           │                                      Change                                      │
     ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │ autocompleter/conversation_extractors.py                 │ Rewrite GeminiExtractor._extract_messages_from_container(), add 3 helper methods │
     ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │ tests/fixtures/expected/gemini-current-chat.json         │ Regenerate via generate_expected.py                                              │
     ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │ tests/fixtures/expected/gemini-browser-current-chat.json │ Regenerate via generate_expected.py                                              │
     └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

     Existing Code to Reuse

     - _collect_child_text() (conversation_extractors.py:58-117) — for extracting text from response content groups
     - ax_get_attribute() (ax_utils.py) — for reading AX attributes from mock elements
     - generate_expected.py — for regenerating golden expected output files
     - Existing _find_landmark_main() and _find_conversation_container() methods — unchanged

     Verification

     1. ./venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_extractor_regression.py -v — all 10 regression tests pass
     2. ./venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v — full test suite passes (480+ tests)
     3. Manually inspect regenerated expected outputs to confirm:
       - No "You said" or "Gemini said" markers in turn text
       - Speaker attribution is correct (User vs Gemini)
       - Gemini response content is captured when available in the AX tree

⏺ Plan approved. Let me set up tasks and start implementing.

      415 +    def _extract_turns_from_pair(                                                                                                                                                         
      416 +        self, element, turns: list[ConversationTurn], max_turns: int,                                                                                                                     
      417 +    ) -> None:                                                                                                                                                                            
      418 +        """Extract User and Gemini turns from a message pair element.                                                                                                                     
      419  
      420 +        Recursively searches for AXHeading elements whose title starts with                                                                                                               
      421 +        ``You said`` or ``Gemini said``, then extracts the appropriate text.

      424 +        self._find_speaker_headings(element, headings, max_depth=12)                                                                                                                      
      425 +

      427 +            if len(turns) >= max_turns:                                                                                                                                                   
      428 +                break                                                                                                                                                                     
      429 +

      431 +                text = self._extract_user_text_from_heading(heading, title)                                                                                                               
      432 +                if text:

      434 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      435 +            elif title.startswith("Gemini said"):                                                                                                                                         
      436 +                text = self._extract_response_after_heading(heading, parent)                                                                                                              
      437 +                if text:                                                                                                                                                                  
      438 +                    turns.append(ConversationTurn(speaker="Gemini", text=text))                                                                                                           
      439 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      440 +    def _find_speaker_headings(                                                                                                                                                           
      441 +        self,                                                                                                                                                                             
      442 +        element,                                                                                                                                                                          
      443 +        results: list[tuple],                                                                                                                                                             
      444 +        max_depth: int,                                                                                                                                                                   
      445 +        depth: int = 0,                                                                                                                                                                   
      446 +        _visits: Optional[list] = None,                                                                                                                                                   
      447 +    ) -> None:                                                                                                                                                                            
      448 +        """Recursively find AXHeading elements with 'You said' or 'Gemini said' titles."""                                                                                                
      449 +        if _visits is None:                                                                                                                                                               
      450 +            _visits = [0]                                                                                                                                                                 
      451 +        _visits[0] += 1                                                                                                                                                                   
      452 +        if _visits[0] > 200 or depth > max_depth:                                                                                                                                         
      453 +            return                                                                                                                                                                        
      454 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      455 +        children = ax_get_attribute(element, "AXChildren")                                                                                                                                
      456 +        if not children:                                                                                                                                                                  
      457 +            return                                                                                                                                                                        
      458 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      459 +        for child in children[:50]:                                                                                                                                                       
      460 +            role = ax_get_attribute(child, "AXRole") or ""                                                                                                                                
      461 +            if role == "AXHeading":                                                                                                                                                       
      462 +                title = (                                                                                                                                                                 
      463 +                    ax_get_attribute(child, "AXTitle")                                                                                                                                    
      464 +                    or ax_get_attribute(child, "AXValue")                                                                                                                                 
      465 +                    or ""                                                                                                                                                                 
      466 +                ).strip()                                                                                                                                                                 
      467 +                if title.startswith("You said") or title.startswith("Gemini said"):                                                                                                       
      468 +                    results.append((child, title, element))                                                                                                                               
      469 +            else:                                                                                                                                                                         
      470 +                self._find_speaker_headings(                                                                                                                                              
      471 +                    child, results, max_depth, depth + 1, _visits,                                                                                                                        
      472 +                )                                                                                                                                                                         
      473 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      474 +    @staticmethod                                                                                                                                                                         
      475 +    def _extract_user_text_from_heading(heading, title: str) -> str:                                                                                                                      
      476 +        """Extract user message text from a 'You said' heading.                                                                                                                           
      477 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      478 +        Prefers the AXGroup child of the heading (which has multi-line content)                                                                                                           
      479 +        over the title-derived text (which may be single-line).                                                                                                                           
      480 +        """                                                                                                                                                                               
      481 +        # Try to get text from the AXGroup child (skipping the "You said" AXStaticText)                                                                                                   
      482 +        children = ax_get_attribute(heading, "AXChildren") or []                                                                                                                          
      483 +        for child in children:                                                                                                                                                            
      484 +            child_role = ax_get_attribute(child, "AXRole") or ""                                                                                                                          
      485 +            if child_role == "AXGroup":                                                                                                                                                   
      486 +                child_text = _collect_child_text(child, max_depth=3, max_chars=1000)                                                                                                      
      487 +                if child_text and child_text.strip():                                                                                                                                     
      488 +                    return child_text.strip()                                                                                                                                             
      489 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      490 +        # Fallback: strip "You said " prefix from the heading title                                                                                                                       
      491 +        prefix = "You said "                                                                                                                                                              
      492 +        if title.startswith(prefix):                                                                                                                                                      
      493 +            return title[len(prefix):].strip()                                                                                                                                            
      494 +        if title == "You said":                                                                                                                                                           
      495 +            return ""                                                                                                                                                                     
      496 +        return ""                                                                                                                                                                         
      497 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      498 +    @staticmethod                                                                                                                                                                         
      499 +    def _extract_response_after_heading(heading, parent) -> str:                                                                                                                          
      500 +        """Extract Gemini response text from siblings following a 'Gemini said' heading."""                                                                                               
      501 +        parent_children = ax_get_attribute(parent, "AXChildren") or []                                                                                                                    
      502 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      503 +        # Find the heading's position among siblings                                                                                                                                      
      504 +        heading_idx = None                                                                                                                                                                
      505 +        for i, child in enumerate(parent_children):                                                                                                                                       
      506 +            if child is heading:                                                                                                                                                          
      507 +                heading_idx = i                                                                                                                                                           
      508 +                break                                                                                                                                                                     
      509 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      510 +        if heading_idx is None:                                                                                                                                                           
      511 +            return ""                                                                                                                                                                     
      512 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      513 +        # Collect text from subsequent siblings until next heading or end                                                                                                                 
      514 +        parts: list[str] = []                                                                                                                                                             
      515 +        for sibling in parent_children[heading_idx + 1:]:                                                                                                                                 
      516 +            sib_role = ax_get_attribute(sibling, "AXRole") or ""                                                                                                                          
      517 +            if sib_role == "AXHeading":                                                                                                                                                   
      518 +                break                                                                                                                                                                     
      519 +            text = _collect_child_text(sibling, max_depth=5, max_chars=1000)                                                                                                              
      520 +            if text and text.strip():                                                                                                                                                     
      521 +                parts.append(text.strip())                                                                                                                                                
      522 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      523 +        return "\n".join(parts)                                                                                                                                                           
      524 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      525 +    @staticmethod                                                                                                                                                                         
      526 +    def _strip_speaker_markers(text: str) -> str:                                                                                                                                         
      527 +        """Strip known Gemini UI speaker markers from text (safety net)."""                                                                                                               
      528 +        text = text.strip()                                                                                                                                                               
      529 +        for prefix in ("You said\n", "Gemini said\n", "You said ", "Gemini said "):                                                                                                       
      530 +            if text.startswith(prefix):                                                                                                                                                   
      531 +                text = text[len(prefix):]                                                                                                                                                 
      532 +        for suffix in ("\nGemini said", "\nYou said"):                                                                                                                                    
      533 +            if text.endswith(suffix):                                                                                                                                                     
      534 +                text = text[: -len(suffix)]                                                                                                                                               
      535 +        return text.strip()                                                                                                                                                               
      536 +                                                                                                                                                                                          
      537 +

⏺ Done. Here's a summary of what was fixed:                                                    
                                                                                               
  Problem: The GeminiExtractor had three issues:             
  1. "You said" / "Gemini said" UI markers leaked into extracted message text                                                                                                                               
  2. Message boundaries were wrong — entire user+response pairs treated as single messages                                                                                                                  
  3. Gemini response content was completely lost                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  Fix: Replaced the index-based alternation loop in _extract_messages_from_container() with heading-based extraction that:                                                                                  
  - Recursively finds AXHeading elements with "You said" / "Gemini said" titles (_find_speaker_headings)
  - Extracts user text from the AXGroup child of "You said" headings, skipping the marker (_extract_user_text_from_heading)
  - Extracts Gemini response text from sibling elements after "Gemini said" headings (_extract_response_after_heading)
  - Falls back to index-based alternation with marker stripping if no headings found

  Results:
  - All 480 tests pass
  - Browser Gemini: 15 clean turns (was 10 corrupted) with full response text
  - Native Gemini: 2 clean user turns without markers
  - ChatGPT, Claude, and all other extractors unaffected

⏺ Now you can run a single command and capture from as many apps as you want:

  source venv/bin/activate && python dump_ax_tree_json.py                                                                                                                                                   
   
  How it works:                                                                                                                                                                                             
  - Runs continuously, listening for Ctrl+Space             
  - Switch to any app window, press Ctrl+Space — it auto-saves to tests/fixtures/ax_trees/<app-name>.json (e.g., google-chrome.json, claude.json, slack.json)
  - If a file already exists for that app, it appends a number: claude-2.json, claude-3.json, etc.
  - Press Ctrl+C in the terminal when done

  You can also specify a different output directory with -d:
  python dump_ax_tree_json.py -d /tmp/ax_dumps
